title: Creamy Sour Malaysian Ginger Soup
description: A comforting, slow‑cooked soup that blends the zing of fresh ginger with the mellow tang of tamarind and the richness of coconut milk, perfect for a cozy evening.

ingredients.name: Lemongrass stalk
ingredients.amount: 1, bruised and cut into 2 cm pieces

instructions: Heat a large pot over medium heat and add a splash of oil.
Sauté the onion until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the garlic, ginger, lemongrass, and red chili flakes; cook until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Stir in the sweet potato cubes and cook for another 2 minutes.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low and let simmer for 25 minutes, or until the sweet potato is tender.
Remove the lemongrass stalks, then stir in the tamarind paste, coconut milk, fish sauce, and lime juice.
Simmer the soup uncovered for an additional 15 minutes to meld flavors, stirring occasionally.
Taste and adjust seasoning with extra fish sauce or lime juice if needed.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped cilantro.
